methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Intramotev is a Series A–stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $14.4M to date. The valuation universe for this space comprises 8 private peers, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No direct public comparables exist in this segment.

Of the two valuation methods tested, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. A Peer-Median approach could not be reliably deployed: while 5 same-sector peers exist, none satisfied narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) required to establish a minimum of 3 datapoints. Applying the Stage-Progression method using total capital raised as a proxy ($14.4M) with a standard Series A–to–subsequent–stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x yields an implied valuation of $29M. This single-method triangulation establishes a median of $29M with a low bound of $24M and high bound of $33M, reflecting a ±15% sensitivity range around the central estimate.

Key uncertainties limiting precision include the absence of narrow peer data at identical stage and valuation tier, which prevents cross-validation via comparable company analysis. The Stage-Progression method's reliance on a standardized 2.0x multiplier assumes typical risk progression from Series A onward but does not account for Intramotev-specific unit economics, technology maturity, or market traction. A valuation range of $24M–$33M is supportable on present data, with $29M as the point estimate.
